title: Living in Boulder vlog 5 - 8 - 2018: Google Earthing Boulder historic photos

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a late-night update on a long-term project where he geolocates historic Boulder, Colorado photos (some ~100 years old) and maps them in a 3D/360 environment. He describes the tedious process of matching old photos to modern locations, dealing with missing buildings, and using Denver Post geo-tagged archives to help place oil derrick photos from around 1902.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Finds it deeply worthwhile despite being a time-consuming 'black hole'
Igniting interest in history is one of the most important things possible, equating it to interest in science
Plans to merge Boulder photos, mountain photos, and Boulder realm into one unified project

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I've been working on a project involving taking photos of Boulder from a hundred years ago and finding where they were taken.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm creating 360-degree views from these old photos if they're near a road or if I can get an actual 360.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to use these 360 views in a cartoon world, including a line that shows the direction and how far you can see in the photo.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: There are 900 photos of Boulder within the town, plus more I haven't counted yet.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I uploaded the photos to Flickr but haven't set them all to public yet because it's a tedious process.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was stuck on photos from the year 1900 for a long time because many were taken by Joseph Sturgan.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm currently working on photos from around 1902, particularly those involving oil derricks.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It's challenging to pinpoint where some of these old photos were taken because the landscape has changed so much.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I found that the Denver Post has locations for some of these photos, which helped me place about 110 of them.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Some photos show houses that aren't on current maps, so I might have to build virtual houses to match the old photos.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: When I successfully line up an old photo with its location, it feels like a victory.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: My goal is to find a plausible location for each photo even if I can't be certain of the exact spot.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm also planning to work on mountain photos and rebuild the mountains in Boulder as they appeared in the past.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm trying to combine this project with two others I'm working on, bouncing between the three to stay productive.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: This project is time-consuming but incredibly interesting because it feels like teleporting back in time.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It's fascinating to think about the generations of people who lived in a completely different Boulder and had great lives.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I believe one of the most important things I can do is ignite interest in history, just like igniting interest in science.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Science and history are interconnected, with science being a form of deep history, like the geologic time scale.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I hope to finish working on the Boulder photos soon and move on to the mountain photos next.
